top_signals.title: SK Hynix Awaits US Debut, Trump Set to Travel to NATO Meeting | The Opening Trade 7/6/2026
top_signals.summary:
This week’s $28 billion US stock-market listing for SK Hynix Inc. may be the biggest-ever first-time share sale by a foreign company, but it isn’t just about raising cash. It’s also about competing in the hottest corner of the global stock market — memory chips used in AI computing.

US President Donald Trump will arrive in Turkey on Tuesday to meet leader Recep Tayyip Erdogan before a NATO summit. Trump will also meet Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy at the defense alliance's gathering in Ankara.

The Opening Trade has everything you need to know as markets open across Europe. With analysis you won't find anywhere else, we break down the biggest stories of the day and speak to top guests who have skin in the game. Hosted by Anna Edwards, Guy Johnson and Tom Mackenzie. (Source: Bloomberg)
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: CNBC Daily Open: Trump sees red, prediction markets score and NATO's defensive
top_signals.summary: The World Cup takes an extraordinary turn after President Trump's reported red card intervention.

top_signals.title: How the Iran crisis undermines the Bank’s QT programme
top_signals.summary: Unlike the previous crises, this time the Bank carried on selling. It is time for a rethink.

top_signals.title: Modi Eyes Stronger Indo-Pacific Ties Amid Mixed US Signals
top_signals.summary: Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi heads to Indonesia, Australia and New Zealand this week to shore up ties with Indo-Pacific nations as questions grow over the US’s commitment to the region and China’s influence expands.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Quant Hedge Funds Extend Worst Run Since 2023 as Momentum Slides
top_signals.summary: A violent rotation in the underbelly of a bullish stock market is extending the worst run for quantitative hedge funds since 2023.
